steps.title: Identify the narrative tense
steps.content: The passage is told entirely in past tense: “first touched down on Mars in January 2004” and “spent over six years gathering information about Mars's stony surface.” The action of naming the rock took place during this completed past period.

steps.title: Choose the past-tense verb form
steps.content: Named is simple past tense, which continues the tense established by “first touched down” and “spent over six years gathering information about Mars's stony surface.” The other choices (names, has named, is naming) all use present-oriented forms, breaking narrative consistency. That is the correct answer.
